Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 5, 300 [sale of slave?][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

⸢f⸣la-tu-ba-⸢šin-ni tar?-ra?⸣ [...] x ⸢SAG?.GEME₂?⸣ x (x)

(o 1) Tattannu, son of Šamašaya, sold Latubašinni ... the slave, to Eriba, son of Kinaya, descendant of Gimil-Nanaya, ... in the month Addaru of the 3rd year of king Xerxes.

o 22

x tar-ra-tu₄ ša₂ ina itiŠE ša₂ MU 3-KAM₂ mḫi-ši-ar-ša₂-šu₂ LUGAL

o 33

mtat-tan-nu DUMU ša₂ mdUTU—a-a a-na KU₃.BABBAR a-na meri-ba

o 44

DUMU ša₂ mki-na-a DUMU mŠU—dna-na-a id-din-nu

o 55

ar₂-ki itiZIZ₂ ša₂ MU 9-KAM₂ mḫi-ši-ar-ša₂-šu₂ LUGAL

(o 5) Later, in the month Šabaṭu of the 9th year of the king Xerxes, Haraṣiannu, son of Bel-iddin, was attending, as witness, at (the writing of the) tablet of completion of that Latubašinni.

o 66

mḫa-ra-ṣi-an-nu DUMU ša₂ mdEN—MU ina imDUB SILIM

o 77

ša₂ fla-tu-ba-šin-ni šu-a-ti₃

o 88

a-na lu₂ši-bu-tu a-ši-ib

Reverse
r 1r 1

lu₂mu-kin-nu mdUTU—KAM DUMU ša₂ m⸢du⸣-muq DUMU mku-⸢ri⸣-[i]

(r 1) Witnesses: Šamaš-ereš, son of Dumuq, descendant of Kuri; Anu-šum-ibni, son of Šuzubu, descendant of Gimil-Nanaya; Guzanu, son of Anu-ahhe-eriba, descendant of Hunzu; Anu-bel-zeri, son of Šamaš-iqišanni, descendant of Šangi-Ninurta; Balaṭu, son of Ardiya, descendant of Kuri; Sin-ahhe-iddin, son of Nadinu, descendant of Kidin-Marduk; Mukkea, son of Kinaya, descendant of Hanbi; Nidintu, son of Kulbibi, descendant of Gimil-Nanaya.

r 22

mda-nu—MU—DU₃ DUMU ša₂ mšu-zu-bu DUMU mŠU—dna-na-⸢a⸣

r 33

mgu-za-nu DUMU ša₂ m⸢d60—ŠEŠ-MEŠ—SU DUMU⸣ mḫu-un-zu-u₂

r 44

md⸢60⸣—EN—NUMUN DUMU ša₂ m⸢dUTU—BAša₂-an-ni DUMU⸣ mSANGA—dMAŠ

r 55

m⸢ba-la⸣-ṭu DUMU ša₂ mARAD-ia DUMU mku-ri-i

r 66

md30—ŠEŠ-MEŠ—MU DUMU ša₂ mna-di-nu DUMU mki-din—dAMAR.UTU

r 77

mmuk-ke-e-a DUMU ša₂ mki-na-a DUMU mḫa-an-bi

r 88

mni-din-tu₄ DUMU ša₂ mkul-bi-bi DUMU m⸢ŠU—dna-na⸣-[a]

(1 line blank)
r 99

lu₂UMBISAG mdUTU—BAša₂-an-ni ⸢DUMU⸣ ša₂ mpir-ʾu DUMU mx—⸢SUM⸣ [...]

(r 9) Scribe: Šamaš-iqišanni son of Pirʾu, descendant of PN [...]. At Uruk, the month Šabaṭu, 21th day, 9th year of Xerxes, king of the Perses, [the Medes, Babylon] and of all the lands.

r 1010

UNUGki itiZIZ₂ U₄ ⸢21?⸣-[KAM₂] ⸢MU 9⸣-KAM₂ mḫi-ši-ar-ša₂-šu₂

r 1111

LUGAL par-su ⸢lu₂⸣[ma-da-a-a E]ki u KUR.KUR
